text.title: Gemini Basics Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for starter Gemini text + chat integrations.

rule.statement: Model is pinned to a specific version (e.g. gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04). Never use "gemini-pro" without a version suffix.
rule.rationale: Vendor aliases shift; pinning protects against silent behaviour drift.

rule.statement: API key comes from an env var or secret manager. Never hard-coded into source; never committed.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded keys leak via git history; standardised env keeps secrets out of code.

rule.statement: Commit to streaming or non-streaming per call site. Do not toggle inside one application path.
rule.rationale: Mixing modes leaks half-streamed tokens to UIs that expect a single response and vice versa.

rule.statement: For typed JSON output, set generation_config.response_mime_type="application/json" and response_schema; do not rely on prompt-only JSON instructions.
rule.rationale: SDK-level mime_type pathway is more reliable than prompt engineering.

rule.statement: Chat history is capped by token budget (target ≤ half the model's context) and trimmed FIFO. Unbounded history hits context limits.
rule.rationale: Unbounded sessions are the leading "AI suddenly fails after 20 turns" support ticket.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire client per templates/gemini-basic-client.py.
Function calling, multimodal, or Vertex enterprise → use sibling methodology.
